retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications
retrieved_docs.content: Craniocervical perforation may develop when the convenience extension is left unfinished before the bur shaft enters the canal space. [63] [61] [62] Pulp debris left behind during preparation may also discolor the crown, especially when the access cavity lacks proper incisal extension and lies too close to the gingival margin. [63] [62] [40] Misjudgment of the tooth's lingual-axial or mesial-axial inclination can result in abrasion of the labial or distal wall during instrumentation. [63] [61] [62] Failure to complete the convenience extension can create a ledge at the apical-labial curve, where the instrument shaft rests on the shoulder or cavity edge instead of advancing smoothly into the canal. [63] [61] [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional technique complications
retrieved_docs.content: Traditional methods for anterior access cavity preparation carry a range of potential complications when executed without sufficient precision. An incomplete convenience extension may produce a pear-shaped apical canal, limiting effective shaping and cleaning. [60] [61] [62] Retention of the lingual shoulder and cavity margin can cause the instrument shaft to rest against these structures, preventing thorough obturation and debridement and ultimately predisposing the case to failure. [40] [61] [62]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: Once the canal orifice is located, the lingual shoulder is eliminated. This dentin shelf extends from the cingulum to approximately 2 mm apical to the orifice. A tapered safety-tip carbide or diamond bur removes this barrier. [38] [57] Proper technique requires placing the fine safety-tip diamond bur about 2 mm apical to the canal orifice and inclining it toward the lingual to slope the shoulder, while avoiding beveling of the incisal edge. Removal of the lingual shoulder produces a continuous, fluid preparation. [38] [54] [56] [59]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Proximal Reduction
retrieved_docs.content: Proximal surfaces are reduced using a 69 L/ tapered fissure bur at high speed to make room for the crown and establish a feather finish line gingivally while avoiding damaging neighboring teeth. The mesial and distal contact points should be cleared so a probe could pass through them. Following the normal proximal contour, proximal slices should slightly converge toward the occlusal and lingual. A smooth taper from occlusal to gingival, free of ledges or shoulders, should be achieved. [10] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: A fissure bur shapes the cavity outline incisally in a funnel or fan configuration. [54] [57] [58] Penetration into the pulp chamber proceeds with a surgical-length No. 2 or No. 4 round bur mounted on a slow-speed contra-angle handpiece. In cases of extensive pulpal recession, a No. 2 bur provides better control. Extension toward the incisal allows the bur shaft to operate nearly parallel to the long axis of the tooth. [38] [59] [57] Following penetration, the remaining tooth structure is removed with a round bur, including dentin beneath the lip of the chamber roof. [38] [59] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Zygomatic Arch Fracture -- Introduction -- Other Landmarks
retrieved_docs.content: The tubercle of Whitnall is the attachment site of the lateral canthal tendon, located on the medial surface of the frontal process of the zygoma.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: With the lingual shoulder cleared and incisal access established, instruments can approach the apical third of the canal without restriction. The prepared cavity reflects the shape of the canal system, supporting round, tapered filling materials placed into the apical third. Circumferential filling instruments or Gates-Glidden drills are then employed to clean and shape the remaining ovoid portion of the canal, ensuring adequate debridement and proper preparation for obturation. [38] [56] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Finishing
retrieved_docs.content: A large green stone is used to create a knife-edge finish at the cervical margin of the crown. The bur is rotated counterclockwise at a 45-degree angle. Then, a rubber wheel is used to smooth out the margins. The crown type can be smoothed and polished with a piece of cloth or chamois wheel on the dental lathe, using Tripoli polishing agent and jeweler's rouge for final finishing. [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: The pulp cavity of anterior teeth is accessed through the exact center of the lingual surface with the traditional method for creating anterior access cavities. [52] Initial entry is performed with a round bur or a round-point tapering fissure bur held at a right angle to the tooth's long axis. [53] A contra-angle handpiece operating at an accelerated speed with water coolant guides this initial penetration. [38] [54] The preparation then extends conveniently toward the incisal, maintaining the bur's point in the central cavity and aligning the handpiece with the tooth's long axis. [55] High-speed instruments should not be used inside the pulp chamber, as they lack the tactile sensitivity required for controlled preparation. [38] [56] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Traditional Methods for Anterior Access Cavity Creation
retrieved_docs.content: In contrast, "adult" teeth exhibit ovoid pulp chambers partially filled with secondary dentin. To enable the bur shaft and instruments to function in the central axis, convenience extension must be advanced further initially when the radiograph shows advanced pulpal recession. The preparation funnels down toward the canal orifice, which becomes increasingly difficult to reach with a round bur as the pulp recedes, often necessitating a deeper initial extension. [56] [59] [57] Such an extension ensures that the bur shaft and instruments remain aligned with the central axis, even in cases with a reduced pulp space. [56] [59] [57]

retrieved_docs.title: Nonsurgical Rhinoplasty Using Fillers -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: The skin should undergo a meticulous disinfection protocol using chlorhexidine gluconate wipes. A topical anesthetic, such as lidocaine, is applied 30 minutes before the procedure. A marking pen can help identify key nasal landmarks, including the midline, nasion, rhinion, and tip-defining points. The clinician may also find it beneficial to outline any contour irregularities (eg, concavities) or other areas that require attention.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Instruments and Materials
retrieved_docs.content: The following instruments are used for the anterior access cavity: General instruments: Endodotic explorer, endodotic excavator, endodotic locking pliers, endodotic ruler, endodotic syringe, instrument organizer, transfer sponge, and instrument stopper. [38] [39] Intracanal instruments: Barbed broach, k-reamers, k-files (k-flex files and headstorm files), spreader, and plugger. [38] [40] Burs used for the anterior access cavity Access opening burs: round burs with 16mm bur shank. [38] [41] Access refining burs: Coarse flame-shaped burs, tapered round, diamonds to refine the wall. [38] [42]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Technique -- Ultraconservative Access Cavity
retrieved_docs.content: The goal of UltraAC is to maintain the maximum amount of tooth structure. The sole goal is to preserve the pericervical dentine, and visibility or straight-line access is frequently jeopardized. A type of UltraAC preparation, known as "ninja access cavities," is created via a "point access" in the central fossa. [43] [68]

retrieved_docs.title: Facial Chin Augmentation -- Preparation
retrieved_docs.content: Gonzalez-Ulloa proposed a line that runs perpendicular to the Frankfort horizontal line and passes through the nasion (the deepest portion of radix). According to this method, the pogonion should either intersect with this line or be situated immediately posterior to it. Furthermore, he classified the degree of insufficiency into 3 grades—grade I if within 1 cm posterior to this line, grade II between 1 and 2 cm posterior, and grade III if greater than 2 cm posterior. Silver proposed a line perpendicular to the Frankfort horizontal line, crossing through the mucocutaneous junction of the lower lip. According to his suggestion, the pogonion should align with this line or, ideally for women, be within 2 mm posterior to it.

retrieved_docs.title: Le Fort Osteotomy -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: The planned osteotomy may be marked with a Bovie electrocautery, a bur, or another implement once fully exposed. The osteotomy should proceed apically to the roots of the teeth while terminating below the inferior turbinate. An instrument, such as a Sayre elevator or freer, may be inserted on the nasal aperture’s medial aspect to protect the previously dissected nasal mucosa while completing the osteotomy if desired.

retrieved_docs.title: Central Line Placement(Archived) -- Technique or Treatment
retrieved_docs.content: Infiltrate the skin with 1% lidocaine for local anesthesia around the needle insertion site.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2402
retrieved_docs.content: Delivery of vertex presentation refers to a vaginal delivery of the fetus when the baby is positioned head‑first, representing a specific type of vaginal delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1702
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN and High risk are both clinically associated with cesarean section, indicating that an unidentified type of cesarean scar and a high‑risk patient status are relevant factors in the consideration, planning, and postoperative management of cesarean deliveries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 3 -- Community 2038
retrieved_docs.content: Previous cesarean section complicating pregnancy or childbirth is recognized as a specific manifestation of a previous c-section, indicating that a prior cesarean delivery can create complications in subsequent pregnancy or childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2341
retrieved_docs.content: The mentum presentation of the fetus, in which the fetal chin is the leading part during delivery, is classified as a مّجيء وَجهي (face presentation). Recognizing this presentation is important for obstetric assessment and delivery planning, as it influences the choice of birth method and the management of potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1802
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with cesarean section, and cesarean section is clinically associated with low transverse cesarean section, indicating that the unknown scar type likely relates to the low transverse incision commonly used in cesarean deliveries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 5 -- Community 2926
retrieved_docs.content: Cesarean section is clinically associated with the low transverse cesarean section technique, and the low transverse cesarean section is in turn clinically associated with early membrane rupture (تمزق الأغشية الباكر). This relationship highlights that the specific low transverse incision used during a cesarean delivery may be linked to an increased likelihood of premature rupture of the fetal membranes, a factor that clinicians consider when planning surgical approaches and managing obstetric risk.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2769
retrieved_docs.content: Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes), indicating that this surgical approach to delivery may be linked to an increased occurrence of early membrane rupture, a factor that clinicians must consider when planning obstetric care and managing potential complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1728
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with the performance of a cesarean section and also with ولادة قبل الأوان (preterm birth). The specific surgical technique of low transverse cesarean section, which is a type of cesarean section, is likewise clinically associated with ولادة قبل الأوان. These connections indicate that both the presence of an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and the use of a low transverse incision may be relevant factors in the risk of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 2 -- Community 752
retrieved_docs.content: essential for clinicians to anticipate potential delivery complications, decide when normal delivery procedures are feasible, and implement appropriate interventions to optimize maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 26 -- Level 3 -- Community 2062
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with both الانفصال المبكر للمشيمة (early placental separation) and مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor). In addition, REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR is clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان, indicating that an unknown‑type cesarean section scar and a poor reproductive history are both risk factors for preterm labor, while the unknown‑type scar also predisposes to early placental separation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2432
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening, unspecified trimester, is mapped to short cervical length in pregnancy, indicating that the condition of cervical shortening at any stage of gestation is understood as a short cervical length—a key obstetric measurement used to evaluate the risk of preterm birth and guide patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 2 -- Community 777
retrieved_docs.content: and uterine scars from previous surgeries, including previous cesarean sections, are specific indications that belong to the larger group of maternal‑care indications triggered by known or suspected pelvic‑organ abnormalities. These indications guide clinical decision‑making throughout pregnancy, labor, and delivery, and they are consistently represented across Dutch and Arabic terminology, ensuring unified understanding and management of maternal health risks.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2393
retrieved_docs.content: External obstetrical version is a fetal procedure that is classified among other delivery procedures, indicating that it is a type of intervention performed on the fetus to reposition it prior to birth and is grouped within the broader category of delivery methods.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1950
retrieved_docs.content: CAESAREAN SECTION SCAR TYPE UNKNOWN is clinically associated with cesarean section. Cesarean section is clinically associated with low transverse cesarean section, a common uterine incision technique. Low transverse cesarean section is clinically associated with تمزق الأغشية الباكر (premature rupture of membranes). Recognizing that an unknown‑type cesarean scar relates to the cesarean procedure, that the procedure often involves a low transverse incision, and that this incision type is linked to premature rupture of membranes helps clinicians anticipate scar‑related issues, plan surgical approaches, and monitor for membrane rupture in patients with current or prior cesarean deliveries.
